reason: Seed mapping. Wikidata P39 (position held) is canonical for role-organization claims about persons; P642 (of) qualifies the organization. Multi-valued: a person holds many positions over time.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P69 (educated at) with degree qualifier P512. Multi-valued: people attend several institutions.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P108 (employer). Multi-valued: many employers over a career.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P463 (member of). Multi-valued: people belong to many groups.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P166 (award received); P585 for point-in-time. Multi-valued: many awards.

Phase G D23 (2026-05-23) corrected from single_valued=0. Residence is user-context information; not resolvable in KB for individuals. user_authoritative. Functional at a point in time: a person has one current residence; relocations are belief-revision events per architecture §6.1. The original value of 0 with the rationale 'single_valued is not consulted on the user-authoritative route' was invalidated by D16 — the walker's object-conflict path and the Tier U write path both consult single_valued on the user_authoritative route. der_revision_001/002 expectations require functional semantics. Phase 10.5 measures the corrected behavior.

Phase H Cluster 3 (2026-05-26) — corrected from P276 to P131. predicate_metadata_corpus/pred_kb_007 pins P131 (located in the administrative territorial entity); the entity_resolution_corpus cases that route through `located_in` (Williams College, Mount Everest, Paris, Eiffel Tower, etc.) all expect institution-in-place semantics, which Wikidata canonicalizes via P131 rather than the more generic P276 (location). The corrected mapping now matches the `located_at` seed (also P131) — both names are surface forms for the same administrative-territorial relationship. Multi-valued: P131 commonly holds several administrative parents (e.g., a city in a county in a state).
Seed mapping. Wikidata P19 (place of birth). Functional: a person has exactly one birthplace.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P20 (place of death). Functional: a person has exactly one place of death.

Seed mapping. Wikidata P569 (date of birth). Functional: a person has exactly one birth date. v0.16 T1 migration: object_type 'date'->'time' so the kb_verifier value-type gate (time:{date,literal}) is live for date predicates.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P570 (date of death). Functional: a person has exactly one death date.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P36 (capital); subject/object inverted to match Aedos claim direction. Functional: a country has one capital in intended use (inverse of has_capital).
Seed mapping. Wikidata P36 (capital); standard direction. Functional: a country has one capital in intended use.

Seed mapping. Wikidata P17 (country). Multi-valued (conservative): P17 is functional for a place, but the predicate name is not subject-type-constrained and a citizenship-style claim could route here, where cardinality is plural (dual citizens).
Seed mapping. Wikidata P30 (continent). Functional: a place is on one continent in intended use (transcontinental edge cases exist).
Seed mapping. Wikidata P26 (spouse); may be user-authoritative for private individuals. Multi-valued: a person may have several spouses over a lifetime.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P40 (child) — subject is parent, object is child. Multi-valued: a parent has many children.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P22 (father); P25 (mother). Seed covers the father slot; mother analogously. Multi-valued: a person has two parents.

Seed mapping. Wikidata P25 (mother); subject/object inverted, so the Aedos subject is the mother. Multi-valued: a mother has many children (the re-audit's 'functional' label read the inverse direction, 'a person's mother').
Seed mapping. Wikidata P3373 (sibling). Multi-valued: a person may have many siblings.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P31 (instance of). Multi-valued: entities have many types.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P279 (subclass of). Used for taxonomic subsumption. Multi-valued: a class may be a subclass of several superclasses.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P27 (country of citizenship). Multi-valued: dual citizens.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P106 (occupation). Multi-valued: a person may have several occupations.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P361 (part of) — mereological relation. Multi-valued: a thing may be part of several wholes.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P527 (has part or parts). Multi-valued.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P527 (has part or parts). Alias for has_part in containment direction. Multi-valued.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P47 (shares border with). Multi-valued: many neighbors.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P1082 (population). Multi-valued: many recorded figures across census years.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P2046 (area). Multi-valued (conservative): land/total/water areas are recorded separately.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P2044 (elevation above sea level). Multi-valued (conservative): highest/lowest/mean points are recorded separately.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P571 (inception/founding date). Functional: an entity has one inception date.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P112 (founded by). Multi-valued: an entity may have several founders.

Phase H Cluster 3 (2026-05-26) — corrected from P585 to P276. P585 is the `point in time` qualifier property and pairs with object_type=date; with object_type=entity the pre-correction mapping was internally inconsistent. The corpus uses `occurred_in` for event-in-location semantics (derivation_corpus der_multihop_003 'The flood occurred in Springfield' + Springfield part_of Illinois → distributes_up; entity_resolution_corpus er_unambiguous_013 'World War II occurred_in <subject>'; predicate_distribution_corpus pd_up_003 pinning the distributes_up over part_of relation). Wikidata P276 (location) is the canonical event-location property. Multi-valued (conservative): events may have multiple recorded locations.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P131 (located in the administrative territorial entity). Multi-valued: P131 commonly holds several administrative parents.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P159 (headquarters location). Multi-valued: an organization may have several headquarters.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P50 (author). Multi-valued: a work may have several authors.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P577 (publication date). Multi-valued (conservative): editions and regional releases carry distinct dates.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P123 (publisher). Multi-valued: a work may have several publishers.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P57 (director). Multi-valued: co-directors.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P577 (publication date). Same property as published_in_year; used for films/albums. Multi-valued (conservative): regional releases carry distinct dates.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P136 (genre). Multi-valued: a work may span several genres.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P407 (language of work or name). Multi-valued: a work may exist in several languages.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P1412 (languages spoken, written or signed). Multi-valued.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P140 (religion or worldview). Multi-valued (conservative): a person's affiliation may change over time.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P102 (member of political party). Multi-valued: people change parties.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P6 (head of government). Functional: one head of government at a time — the LLM-generated valid_from/valid_until qualifiers carry the over-time change.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P35 (head of state). Functional: one head of state at a time — the LLM-generated valid_from/valid_until qualifiers carry the over-time change.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P61 (discoverer or inventor). Multi-valued: co-discoverers.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P61 (discoverer or inventor). Alias for discovered_by. Multi-valued: co-inventors.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P156 (followed by). Multi-valued (conservative): branching sequences admit several successors.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P155 (follows). Multi-valued (conservative): branching sequences admit several predecessors.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P38 (currency). Multi-valued (conservative): some countries have several legal-tender currencies.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P137 (operator). Multi-valued: a facility may have several operators.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P127 (owned by). Multi-valued: shared ownership.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P6104 (maintained by WikiProject) — approximate; deployment may refine. Multi-valued.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P1128 (employees). Multi-valued: many figures across reporting years.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P2139 (total revenue). Multi-valued: many figures across reporting years.
Seed mapping. Wikidata P21 (sex or gender). Functional: P21 carries a single sex-or-gender value per subject.

Phase G D39 (2026-05-23). Wikidata P569 (date of birth), year component only. Functional: a person has one birth date. Used by der_cross_007 (Tier U provides the year; arithmetic verification fires on a separate computed-predicate claim that consumes this value). Entity types: subject=human (Q5), object=year (Q577).

Phase G D39 (2026-05-23). Preferences are user-authoritative; the asserting party is ground truth. Functional at a point in time (one current preference per object-class for the same subject), revisable over time (der_revision_001 'Asa prefers tea' → 'Asa prefers coffee' = contradicted). Matches predicate_metadata_corpus pred_ua_001 pinned metadata exactly. Entity-types omitted: prefers takes objects of many types (food, brand, color, etc.) — type filter would over-constrain.

Phase G D39 (2026-05-23). Status (project/marriage/role/etc.) is user-authoritative; the asserting party tracks it. Functional: one current status at a time, revisable (der_revision_005 'project status ongoing' + 'The project ended in 2024' = contradicted). Entity-types omitted: status values are state labels (e.g. 'ongoing', 'ended'), not Wikidata-resolvable entities. v0.16 candidate: re-evaluate if corpus additions introduce multiple senses (project status vs marital status vs employment status), in which case split into status_project / status_marital / status_employment.

v0.16 T1. Employment start date = P580 (start time) qualifier on the P108 (employer) statement. The object is the date; it grounds against the qualifier, not the statement value, so the walker's interval resolver (routing_hint=kb_interval) reads P580 off the base P108 statement rather than the generic value-compare path. Multi-valued: a person may have several employments, each with its own start. The org slot maps to statement_value so the resolver can pick the matching employer statement.

v0.16 T1. Employment end date = P582 (end time) qualifier on the P108 (employer) statement. Object is the date; grounds against the qualifier via the kb_interval resolver. Multi-valued: several employments, each with its own end.
v0.16 T1. Membership start date = P580 (start time) qualifier on the P463 (member of) statement. kb_interval resolver reads P580 off the base P463 statement. Multi-valued.
v0.16 T1. Membership end date = P582 (end time) qualifier on the P463 (member of) statement. kb_interval resolver. Multi-valued.

v0.16 T1. Role/position start date = P580 (start time) qualifier on the P39 (position held) statement. kb_interval resolver reads P580 off the base P39 statement. Multi-valued: a person may hold several positions, each with its own start.
v0.16 T1. Role/position end date = P582 (end time) qualifier on the P39 (position held) statement. kb_interval resolver. Multi-valued.

v0.16 T1. State-bearing-subject start = inception (P571). Unlike the employment/membership/role endpoints, inception/dissolution are STATEMENTS, not qualifiers, but the kb_interval resolver also gathers a Tier U started/ended row; for an org subject prefer founded_in_year (P571) / dissolved_in_year (P576) directly. Object is the date. Multi-valued (conservative).
v0.16 T1. State-bearing-subject end = dissolution (P576). See status_started. Object is the date. Multi-valued (conservative).
